perturbation.original_text: 4.4 Intricon is entitled to credit for any amounts payable to Dynamic Hearing arising from per unit royalties and fees for services up to the value of the corresponding quarterly Second Component. In quarters where the per unit royalties and fees for services are less than the corresponding quarterly Second Component, IntriCon may carry forward the remaining credit to be offset against future quarters per unit royalties and fees for services. Any amounts due from per unit royalties and fees for services that exceed the value of the corresponding quarterly payment of the Second Component, net of any carried forward credit, will be paid within 30 days of the end of that royalty quarter.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.4 Intricon is entitled to credit for any amounts payable to Dynamic Hearing arising from per unit royalties and fees for services up to the value of the corresponding quarterly Second Component. In quarters where the per unit royalties and fees for services are less than the corresponding quarterly Second Component, IntriCon may carry forward the remaining credit to be offset against future quarters per unit royalties and fees for services for a period not exceeding one year. Any amounts due from per unit royalties and fees for services that exceed the value of the corresponding quarterly payment of the Second Component, net of any carried forward credit, will be paid within 60 days of the end of that royalty quarter.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the payment terms. Originally, IntriCon was entitled to carry forward remaining credit indefinitely, however the modified term restricts the carry forward to one year. Furthermore, the original stated that 'Any amounts due... will be paid within 30 days', however the modified term states that 'Any amounts due...will be paid within 60 days'. This contradicts with the clause 4.4 and creates uncertainty about the payment deadline.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.4 Intricon is entitled to credit for any amounts payable to Dynamic Hearing arising from per unit royalties and fees for services up to the value of the corresponding quarterly Second Component. In quarters where the per unit royalties and fees for services are less than the corresponding quarterly Second Component, IntriCon may carry forward the remaining credit to be offset against future quarters per unit royalties and fees for services for a period not exceeding one year. Any amounts due from per unit royalties and fees for services that exceed the value of the corresponding quarterly payment of the Second Component, net of any carried forward credit, will be paid within 60 days of the end of that royalty quarter.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text allowed IntriCon to carry forward remaining credit indefinitely, while the changed text limits this to one year, which is also present in the contradicted text. Furthermore, the payment deadline was extended from 30 to 60 days in the changed text which directly contradicts the clause 4.4, also found in the contradicted text. Therefore the changed text introduces a contradiction according to the inconsistencies definition.

perturbation.original_text: 3.11 Where Dynamic Hearing supplies Dynamic Hearing Technology directly to a customer on DSP chips supplied by IntriCon, then, IntriCon will use its best efforts to comply with the request for HIMPP compliance or notify the third party customer of the customers obligations, as per 3.10 (a) and (b).
perturbation.changed_text: 3.11 Where Dynamic Hearing supplies Dynamic Hearing Technology directly to a customer on DSP chips supplied by IntriCon, then, Dynamic Hearing will be solely responsible to comply with the request for HIMPP compliance or notify the third party customer of the customers obligations, as per 3.10 (a) and (b).
perturbation.explanation: This modification creates an in-text contradiction by shifting the responsibility for HIMPP compliance from IntriCon to Dynamic Hearing. The original text states IntriCon will use best efforts to comply, however the modified text states that Dynamic Hearing will be solely responsible. This creates uncertainty regarding which party is ultimately responsible for HIMPP compliance, and contradicts clause 3.11.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.11 Where Dynamic Hearing supplies Dynamic Hearing Technology directly to a customer on DSP chips supplied by IntriCon, then, Dynamic Hearing will be solely responsible to comply with the request for HIMPP compliance or notify the third party customer of the customers obligations, as per 3.10 (a) and (b).
perturbation.justification: The changed text shifts the responsibility for HIMPP compliance from IntriCon to Dynamic Hearing. The original text states IntriCon will use best efforts to comply, while the modified text states that Dynamic Hearing will be solely responsible. This creates a direct conflict regarding who is accountable for HIMPP compliance. The Change Text and Contradicted Text are identical, meaning the contradiction occurs due to the modification introduced in the Changed Text, as per the explanation. According to the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', this is a strong contradiction because it provides conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 3.2 Dynamic Hearing grants to IntriCon in accordance with this Agreement, for the Term, a license, to Use Dynamic Hearing's Technology, Software and Documentation developed as of the Commencement Date to manufacture, import, sell and offer for sale throughout the Territory, Products containing Dynamic Hearing's Technology and Software. This grant specifically excludes products for Contact Centers, and the use of ADRO Technology in electrical stimulation of the auditory system.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 Dynamic Hearing grants to IntriCon in accordance with this Agreement, for a period of two years from the Commencement Date, a license, to Use Dynamic Hearing's Technology, Software and Documentation developed as of the Commencement Date to manufacture, import, sell and offer for sale throughout the Territory, Products containing Dynamic Hearing's Technology and Software. This grant specifically excludes products for Contact Centers, and the use of ADRO Technology in electrical stimulation of the auditory system.
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces an in-text contradiction by restricting the license grant for a period of two years. The original text stated that the license was granted for the 'Term', which is defined in section 3.1 as five years. This creates a contradiction between the term of the license and the term of the agreement, which leads to uncertainty regarding the duration of the license grant.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 Term: The initial term of this Agreement shall be five (5) years from the date of execution and may be extended subject to satisfactory agreement on ongoing commercial terms, to be agreed two (2) months prior to the expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 3.2 to limit the license grant to two years from the commencement date, directly contradicting section 3.1 which states the agreement has an initial term of five years. This creates a clear inconsistency regarding the duration of the license, as the original text granted the license for the entire 'Term' (five years), while the modified text shortens it to two years, directly violating the explicit term stated in section 3.1.
